def solve(nums, k)
    n = nums.size
    return 0 if n == 0
    nums.sort!
    max_len = 1
    for i in 0...n do
        target = nums[i] * k
        low = i
        high = n - 1
        best = i - 1
        while low <= high do
            mid = (low + high) / 2
            if nums[mid] <= target
                best = mid
                low = mid + 1
            else
                high = mid - 1
            end
        end
        if best >= i
            current_len = best - i + 1
            max_len = [max_len, current_len].max
        end
    end
    return n - max_len
end
```

Now, the code needs to be written in C++. Also, note that in C++, the upper_bound function is in the <algorithm> header. So the code should include that.

So the code will be:

#include <algorithm>
#include <vector>

long long solve(std::vector<long long> nums, long long k) {
    int n = nums.size();
    if (n == 0) return 0;
    std::sort(nums.begin(), nums.end());
    long long max_len = 1;
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        long long target = nums[i] * k;
        auto it = std::upper_bound(nums.begin() + i, nums.end(), target);
        int j = it - nums.begin() - 1;
        if (j >= i) {
            int current_len = j - i + 1;
            if (current_len > max_len) {
                max_len = current_len;
            }
        }
    }
    return n - max_len;
}
